contract.claim_boundary: Host-side NEST diagnostic of bounded signal transport only. Not mechanism promotion, not baseline freeze, not hardware evidence.

conditions.export_disabled: Full lifecycle stack + raw spike transport (current behavior, sham)
conditions.export_enabled: Full lifecycle stack + bounded export interface active

contract.diagnosis:
Current inter-polyp communication transmits raw spikes through PyNN projections — an unbounded channel where any polyp's internal state can destabilize neighbors.  This causes the accumulating NEST failures we observe at scale.

Biological systems use compressive signaling: neurons export action potentials (binary), cells export hormones (bounded concentration), organs export blood chemistry (filtered). The polyp should export a bounded interface vector:
  [activity, prediction, uncertainty, energy, novelty]
with contractive transport (tanh/sigmoid bounds, spectral normalization) so that messages cannot amplify divergence.
contract.hypothesis: Adding a bounded export interface per polyp — with tanh/sigmoid bounds on all exported values — will maintain or improve PR while reducing NEST failure rate by providing a compressive transport layer that prevents signal amplification.

mechanism_under_test.name: signal_transport_export
mechanism_under_test.transport_constraint: All exported values pass through tanh or sigmoid bounds. Inter-polyp messages carry this compressed vector.

contract.null_hypothesis: Bounded export does not improve stability because: (1) the NEST failures are from connection accumulation, not signal divergence, (2) the per-polyp interface adds overhead without value, or (3) existing ReefNetwork already provides sufficient coupling.

outcome_classes.action: Bounded export interface preserves diversity while improving stability.
Export helps quality but doesn't reduce NEST platform issues.
Bounded export adds overhead without benefit at this scale.
outcome_classes.condition: No difference in PR or failures

pass_criteria.primary: export_enabled PR > export_disabled PR by >0.5 AND export_enabled NEST failures < export_disabled failures AND population remains stable (no crashes)

contract.question: Does a bounded per-polyp export interface (activity, prediction, uncertainty, energy, novelty) with contractive transport constraints preserve rich internal dynamics (PR maintained) while preventing cross-polyp signal amplification that degrades stability?

tier: Tier 5.38 — Signal Transport: Bounded Export Interface Contract
